flashcards.back: The process of finding a substitution that makes two logical expressions syntactically identical.
A finite set of variable-term pairs of the form {v1/t1, v2/t2, ..., vn/tn}, where each vi is a distinct variable and each ti is a term not equal to vi.

flashcards.front: What is the Most General Unifier (MGU)?
flashcards.back: A unifier for two expressions such that all other unifiers are instances of it. It imposes the fewest constraints on the variables.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the 'Occurs Check'?
flashcards.back: To prevent infinite unification by ensuring a variable is not unified with a term that contains that same variable (e.g., x and f(x)).

flashcards.front: Name two inference procedures that use unification.
